input.headline: Why Labour MPs won't be comfortable with this approach

raw.headline: Why Labour MPs won't be comfortable with this approach

winner: Why did the Labour MPs avoid a hug from their new chair? Because they found his "approach" a bit too "close-quartered"!

candidates.text: Apparently, they've started reupholstering the House of Commons benches in cushions filled with hot potatoes. Labour MPs won't be comfortable with this approach, I suppose.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist with a clever pun on 'hot potatoes'"}

candidates.text: Why did the Labour MPs avoid a hug from their new chair? Because they found his "approach" a bit too "close-quartered"!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'close-quartered,' but it's a bit forced and relies heavily on political insider knowledge, limiting its general appeal."}

candidates.text: Why are Labour MPs uneasy about this new seating arrangement? Because they can't stand being on their knees!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the pun on 'on their knees' feels a bit forced and lacks natural flow. It's not offensive, but it misses the mark slightly in terms of humor impact."}

candidates.text: A parliamentarian, feeling a bit chilly, decides to adopt a unique solution: he brings his own sheep to parliament, reasoning that "Labour MPs won't be uncomfortable with this approach.
